title: podman dose not run with --init and libkrun
body:
### Issue Description

Podman dose not run with `init` option and libkrun OCI handler.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. install podman, libkrun, libkrunfw in fedora 39 `dnf install podman libkrun libkrunfw`
2. run podman with init option and libkrun `podman run --annotation=run.oci.handler=krun --init --rm --init docker.io/library/busybox uname -a`


### Describe the results you received

Dose not run a container with libkrun and `--init` option.

```console
root@fedora:~# podman run --annotation=run.oci.handler=krun --init --rm docker.io/library/busybox uname -a
ERROR (catatonit:1): missing program name
usage: catatonit [-ghLPV] [--] <progname> [<arguments>...]
```

### Describe the results you expected

Run contiainer like without init option.

```console
root@fedora:~# podman run --rm --annotation=run.oci.handler=krun docker.io/library/busybox uname -a
Linux 98c7280beb96 6.2.14 #1 SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Tue May  9 05:06:07 PM CEST 2023 x86_64 GNU/Linux
root@fedora:~# podman run --rm  docker.io/library/busybox uname -a
Linux bcb06d1692f1 6.6.7-200.fc39.x86_64 #1 SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Wed Dec 13 21:43:37 UTC 2023 x86_64 GNU/Linux
root@fedora:~# podman run --rm --init docker.io/library/busybox uname -a
Linux ff227e967e82 6.6.7-200.fc39.x86_64 #1 SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Wed Dec 13 21:43:37 UTC 2023 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Privileged

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

Package information

```
root@fedora:~# rpm -q podman
podman-4.8.1-1.fc39.x86_64
root@fedora:~# rpm -qa | grep  libkrun
libkrunfw-3.12.0-2.fc39.x86_64
libkrun-1.5.0-5.fc39.x86_64
```

Run fedora 39 vm on Hyper-V. But I enable [Nested Virtualization](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/virtualization/hyper-v-on-windows/user-guide/nested-virtualization)

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body: @slp In order to get this to run, we would need to volume mount in /usr/libexec/podman/catatonit into the VM and run it.  The issue is we do not handle the init problem.

I try some test cases. These test cases reveal problems with the handling of command-line arguments.

### case1

```Dockerfile
FROM docker.io/library/busybox:glibc
ENTRYPOINT ["/bin/echo", "--"]
CMD ["uname", "-a"]
```

```console
root@fedora:~/test# podman build -t localhost/test .
STEP 1/3: FROM docker.io/library/busybox:glibc
STEP 2/3: ENTRYPOINT ["/bin/echo", "--"]
--> Using cache 3e2f6a2cb9f75015d92714761e21f5470e6a8a8d6da8a0eebe9a83e125511732
--> 3e2f6a2cb9f7
STEP 3/3: CMD ["uname", "-a"]
--> Using cache 888d180df4487fe655042bd3037706151439537db137469320e517b7a503aac2
COMMIT localhost/test
--> 888d180df448
Successfully tagged localhost/test:latest
888d180df4487fe655042bd3037706151439537db137469320e517b7a503aac2
root@fedora:~/test# podman run --rm --annotation=run.oci.handler=krun localhost/test:latest

root@fedora:~/test# podman run --rm localhost/test:latest
-- uname -a
```

### case 2

```Dockerfile
FROM docker.io/library/busybox:glibc
CMD ["/bin/echo", "--", "uname", "-a"]
```

```console
root@fedora:~/test# podman build -t localhost/test .
STEP 1/2: FROM docker.io/library/busybox:glibc
STEP 2/2: CMD ["/bin/echo", "--", "uname", "-a"]
COMMIT localhost/test
--> 93961aceeaaf
Successfully tagged localhost/test:latest
93961aceeaafbbcbbd43dce18b72549747ba20b2eca3769f4279c10f2ade5196
root@fedora:~/test# podman run --rm localhost/test:latest
-- uname -a
root@fedora:~/test# podman run --rm --annotation=run.oci.handler=krun localhost/test:latest
```

So runc and crun both output the correct content, it is only krun which seems to eat the output.

If I remove the "--" from the Entrypoint it seems to work ok, so the "--" seems to be the issue.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

The underlying problem is that podman is adding a "--" to the args that's messing with libkrun's argument passing through the kernel's command line. We could fix this specific issue, but I think it's actually better to update crun to stop passing the arguments through the command line, relying on the OCI definition put together by podman instead.

I'll prepare a PR for crun doing that.
